items.title: Fox says the Argentina-England game averaged 15.06 million viewers. Most watched World Cup semifinal in U.S. TV history. Excellent number.
items.author: Richard Deitsch
items.body_excerpt:
Fox says the Argentina-England game averaged 15.06 million viewers. Most watched World Cup semifinal in U.S. TV history. Excellent number.

Previous high for the men: France-Morocco averaged 6.59 million viewers in 2022. (Caveats are time of day (2PM ET kick in 2022) and new Nielsen methodology.)

items.title: ESPN is interested in acquiring NBA's eventual centralized local broadcast package. Jimmy Pitaro, at CNBC/Boardroom event, said he wants ESPN to help fix local TV issue.
items.author: Mike Vorkunov
items.body_excerpt:
ESPN is interested in acquiring NBA's eventual centralized local broadcast package. Jimmy Pitaro, at CNBC/Boardroom event, said he wants ESPN to help fix local TV issue.

"We've made our intentions very clear with with every league, including the NBA, that we want to be part of the solution here."

items.title: The Miami Heat and Vlad Goldin have agreed to a two-way deal, league sources told HoopsHype. Goldin played in nine games for the Heat last season. He averaged 12.3 points on 59.2% shooting from the field, 7.6 rebounds, and 1.9 blocks, with Sioux Falls, Miami’s G League affiliate.

items.body_excerpt: The Miami Heat and Vlad Goldin have agreed to a two-way deal, league sources told HoopsHype. Goldin played in nine games for the Heat last season. items.title: Bonus Hollander POTD Connie Hawkins. NYC legend got caught up in mostly BS scandal that shortened his career. Played in EBL & was MVP of early ABA (67-69). Did well for PHO from 69-72 but declined after. Finished up w/LA & ATL thru 76. Passed away in 2017. RIP to this HOFer.
items.author: HoopsAnalyst

items.title: One thing I always think about with the rigged crowd: If you actually believe in good faith that the 2020 U.S. Election was rigged, then logically you should be calling out the results of Senate elections in Idaho, Iowa, Kansas, Louisana and on and on, no?
items.author: Richard Deitsch
items.body_excerpt:
One thing I always think about with the rigged crowd: If you actually believe in good faith that the 2020 U.S. Election was rigged, then logically you should be calling out the results of Senate elections in Idaho, Iowa, Kansas, Louisana and on and on, no? 

But stunningly I never see this.
